clusters.title: Trump meets with Chinese leader Xi in Beijing
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump, Xi speak ahead of talks to make relations ‘better than ever’
bullet_summary: President Trump said before he left that he and President Xi Jinping "have a lot of things to discuss
Reported by CBS News, Al Jazeera, NBC World and others

items.title: Trump meets with Chinese leader Xi in Beijing
items.standfirst: President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping met in Beijing, as the two countries aim to stabilize their trading relationship and grapple with uncertainty over Iran.
items.content: President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping met in Beijing, as the two countries aim to stabilize their trading relationship and grapple with uncertainty over Iran.

items.title: Trump, Xi speak ahead of talks to make relations ‘better than ever’
items.standfirst: Presidents Xi Jinping and Donald Trump say they’re looking forward to US-China talks.
items.content: Presidents Xi Jinping and Donald Trump say they’re looking forward to US-China talks.

items.title: Xi Jinping welcomes Donald Trump with ceremony ahead of high-stakes meeting
items.standfirst: Chinese President Xi Jinping is welcoming US President Donald Trump to Beijing for high-level talks. Tariffs, competitio
items.content: Chinese President Xi Jinping is welcoming US President Donald Trump to Beijing for high-level talks. Tariffs, competitio

items.title: ‘We should be partners, not rivals’: Chinese President Xi Jinping opens bilateral talks
items.standfirst: President Xi Jinping opened the bilateral talks with President Trump by emphasizing that a stable bilateral relationship benefits the world and stating, “We should be partners, not rivals.”
items.content: President Xi Jinping opened the bilateral talks with President Trump by emphasizing that a stable bilateral relationship benefits the world and stating, “We should be partners, not rivals.”

items.title: Watch: Trump, Xi arrive for summit amid pomp and circumstance | Special Report
items.standfirst: President Trump and Xi Jinping arrived for their high-stakes summit Thursday morning in Beijing. They are expected to discuss trade, Taiwan and the war with Iran. Jessi Mitchell anchors CBS News' special report.
items.content: President Trump and Xi Jinping arrived for their high-stakes summit Thursday morning in Beijing. They are expected to discuss trade, Taiwan and the war with Iran. Jessi Mitchell anchors CBS News' special report.

items.title: China's Xi promises more trade but warns Trump over Taiwan as summit kicks off in Beijing
items.standfirst: China's President Xi Jinping told his US counterpart Donald Trump that Chinese markets will be more open to US and global trade in the future as a two-day summit got under way in Beijing on Thursday but warned that persistent disagreements over Taiwan could send relations down a dangerous path.
items.content: China's President Xi Jinping told his US counterpart Donald Trump that Chinese markets will be more open to US and global trade in the future as a two-day summit got under way in Beijing on Thursday but warned that persistent disagreements over Taiwan could send relations down a dangerous path.

items.title: How Taiwan factors into Trump's meeting with Xi Jinping
items.standfirst: President Trump is in Beijing for a high-stakes meeting with his Chinese counterpart, Xi Jinping. He last visited Beijing nearly a decade ago in 2017. This time around, the two are expected to discuss trade, the Middle East and Taiwan. Jonathan Czin, a former senior China analyst at the CIA, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.
items.content: President Trump is in Beijing for a high-stakes meeting with his Chinese counterpart, Xi Jinping. He last visited Beijing nearly a decade ago in 2017. This time around, the two are expected to discuss trade, the Middle East and Taiwan. Jonathan Czin, a former senior China analyst at the CIA, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

items.title: Trump arrives in Beijing to meet with Xi Jinping
items.standfirst: President Trump said before he left that he and President Xi Jinping "have a lot of things to discuss."
items.content: President Trump said before he left that he and President Xi Jinping "have a lot of things to discuss."

items.title: How improved U.S.-China relationship could benefit the world
items.standfirst: President Trump arrived in Beijing on Wednesday morning ahead of a high-stakes meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping. CBS News' Anna Coren and Natalie Brand report.
items.content: President Trump arrived in Beijing on Wednesday morning ahead of a high-stakes meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping. CBS News' Anna Coren and Natalie Brand report.

items.title: Trade, Iran and Taiwan on the agenda as Trump arrives in China for high-stakes talks with Xi
items.standfirst: The US president says his first request to Xi would be to "open up" China, while Beijing is expected to press Trump on the status of Taiwan.
items.content: The US president says his first request to Xi would be to "open up" China, while Beijing is expected to press Trump on the status of Taiwan.

items.title: Trade, Taiwan and Iran cast shadows on Trump’s China summit with Xi
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump will begin a two-day summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing on Thursday as the two countries engage in a fierce trade war and the U.S. conflict with Iran rages on.
items.content: President Donald Trump will begin a two-day summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing on Thursday as the two countries engage in a fierce trade war and the U.S. conflict with Iran rages on.

items.title: Russia pounds Kyiv and cities across Ukraine in massive air attack
items.standfirst: Russia pummeled the Ukrainian capital Kyiv and ​several other regions in a massive aerial attack involving drones and ballistic missiles, killing at least one person and wounding dozens, local officials said Thursday.
items.content: Russia pummeled the Ukrainian capital Kyiv and ​several other regions in a massive aerial attack involving drones and ballistic missiles, killing at least one person and wounding dozens, local officials said Thursday.

items.title: Russia hits Kyiv with drones and ballistic missiles, killing 1 and injuring 31
items.standfirst: Russia has launched a mass drone and missile attack on Ukraine's capital, Kyiv, killing one person and injuring at least 31. Local authorities report damage across six districts.
items.content: Russia has launched a mass drone and missile attack on Ukraine's capital, Kyiv, killing one person and injuring at least 31. Local authorities report damage across six districts.(Image credit: Evgeniy Maloletka)

items.title: Kyiv building collapses after Russian strike, reportedly trapping residents

items.content: Russia attacked the Ukrainian capital Kyiv with drones and missiles, damaging several buildings.

items.title: Russia unleashes massive drone barrage on Ukraine killing six
items.standfirst: Russia has launched a massive daytime drone barrage across Ukraine, killing at least six people.
items.content: Russia has launched a massive daytime drone barrage across Ukraine, killing at least six people.

items.title: Russia unleashes deadly daylight drone blitz across Ukraine
items.standfirst: A sweeping daytime Russian drone assault killed at least six people and wounded dozens across Ukraine on Wednesday, marking a shift in Moscow’s bombardment tactics. Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelensky said more than 800 drones were launched, accusing Russia of timing the attack to coincide with US President Donald Trump’s visit to China.
items.content: A sweeping daytime Russian drone assault killed at least six people and wounded dozens across Ukraine on Wednesday, marking a shift in Moscow’s bombardment tactics. Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelensky said more than 800 drones were launched, accusing Russia of timing the attack to coincide with US President Donald Trump’s visit to China.

items.title: Deadly Russian drone attacks on Ukraine resume after ceasefire expires
items.standfirst: Six people have been killed after Zelensky warned of "more waves" of Russian strikes through Wednesday.
items.content: Six people have been killed after Zelensky warned of "more waves" of Russian strikes through Wednesday.

items.title: Russia targets Ukraine with more than 800 drones in deadly daytime assault
items.standfirst: Strikes killed at least six people as Moscow and Kyiv trade long-range attacks after brief ceasefireEurope live – latest updatesRussia targeted Ukraine with more than 800 drones in a large-scale daytime assault that killed at least six people on Wednesday, hours after a previous deadly barrage.The strikes came as Kyiv and Moscow traded long-range attacks after a brief ceasefire, and despite the latest suggestion from Donald Trump that the war could soon come to an end. Continue reading...
items.content: Strikes killed at least six people as Moscow and Kyiv trade long-range attacks after brief ceasefireEurope live – latest updatesRussia targeted Ukraine with more than 800 drones in a large-scale daytime assault that killed at least six people on Wednesday, hours after a previous deadly barrage.The strikes came as Kyiv and Moscow traded long-range attacks after a brief ceasefire, and despite the latest suggestion from Donald Trump that the war could soon come to an end. clusters.title: Remains of 2nd U.S. soldier who went missing in Morocco have been recovered
clusters.neutral_headline: Remains of 2nd U.S. soldier who went missing in Morocco have been recovered
bullet_summary: The soldier was identified as Spc Mariyah Symone Collington of Taveres, Florida, the US Army Europe and Africa said in a statement
Reported by NPR News, CBS News, Guardian World

items.title: Remains of 2nd U.S. soldier who went missing in Morocco have been recovered
items.standfirst: Spc. Mariyah Symone Collington, 19, of Taveres, Florida, is the second U.S. soldier who fell off a cliff during a recreational hike in Morocco. The remains of 1st Lt. Kendrick Lamont Key Jr. were recovered last week.
items.content: Spc. Mariyah Symone Collington, 19, of Taveres, Florida, is the second U.S. soldier who fell off a cliff during a recreational hike in Morocco. The remains of 1st Lt. Kendrick Lamont Key Jr. were recovered last week.(Image credit: Mosa'ab Elshamy)

items.title: Body of second U.S. soldier who went missing in Morocco recovered
items.standfirst: The Army identified the soldier as Spc. Mariyah Collington.
items.content: The Army identified the soldier as Spc. Mariyah Collington.

items.title: Remains of second US soldier who went missing during military exercises in Morocco have been recovered
items.standfirst: Mariyah Symone Collington and Kendrick Lamont Key Jr, who also died, had fallen off a cliff during an off-duty hikeThe remains of the second US army soldier who went missing during military exercises in Morocco have been recovered, the army said on Wednesday, ending a multinational search operation that deployed air, naval and artificial intelligence assets.The soldier was identified as Spc Mariyah Symone Collington of Taveres, Florida, the US Army Europe and Africa said in a statement. She was 19 years old. Continue reading...
items.content: Mariyah Symone Collington and Kendrick Lamont Key Jr, who also died, had fallen off a cliff during an off-duty hikeThe remains of the second US army soldier who went missing during military exercises in Morocco have been recovered, the army said on Wednesday, ending a multinational search operation that deployed air, naval and artificial intelligence assets.The soldier was identified as Spc Mariyah Symone Collington of Taveres, Florida, the US Army Europe and Africa said in a statement. She was 19 years old. Continue reading...

items.content: Three years after Alex Murdaugh was convicted of killing his wife and son, the South Carolina Supreme Court on Wednesday overturned the verdict and granted Murdaugh a new trial. CBS News' Katrina Kaufman reports.

clusters.title: Senate confirms Trump pick Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve chair
clusters.neutral_headline: Senate confirms Trump pick Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve chair
bullet_summary: The US Senate on Wednesday confirmed Kevin Warsh – Trump's top pick – as the new Federal Reserve chair in the most partisan vote ever for the role
Reported by France24, Guardian Business, NPR News

items.title: Senate confirms Trump pick Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve chair
items.standfirst: The US Senate on Wednesday confirmed Kevin Warsh – Trump's top pick – as the new Federal Reserve chair in the most partisan vote ever for the role. Warsh takes over as policymakers warn persistent price pressures could complicate President Donald Trump’s push for lower borrowing costs.
items.content: The US Senate on Wednesday confirmed Kevin Warsh – Trump's top pick – as the new Federal Reserve chair in the most partisan vote ever for the role. Warsh takes over as policymakers warn persistent price pressures could complicate President Donald Trump’s push for lower borrowing costs.

items.title: US Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve chair, replacing Jerome Powell
items.standfirst: Warsh will serve four-year term as chair, taking over amid rising inflation and pressure from Trump to lower ratesThe US Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh as chair of the Federal Reserve, one of the most powerful roles in the federal government that holds enormous sway over the economy.The 54-45 Senate vote on Wednesday was split along party lines, with the exception of the Democratic senator John Fetterman from Pennsylvania, who joined the Republican majority. It was most divisive confirmation vote for the position in history. Continue reading...
items.content: Warsh will serve four-year term as chair, taking over amid rising inflation and pressure from Trump to lower ratesThe US Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh as chair of the Federal Reserve, one of the most powerful roles in the federal government that holds enormous sway over the economy.The 54-45 Senate vote on Wednesday was split along party lines, with the exception of the Democratic senator John Fetterman from Pennsylvania, who joined the Republican majority. It was most divisive confirmation vote for the position in history. Continue reading...

items.title: Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as next chair of the Federal Reserve
items.standfirst: Warsh has argued there's room for the central bank to lower interest rates, but that could be challenging at a time of rising inflation.
items.content: Warsh has argued there's room for the central bank to lower interest rates, but that could be challenging at a time of rising inflation. (Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
